The Director of Engineering Operations and Strategy will serve as a key member of the engineering leadership team, responsible for driving operational excellence, organizational effectiveness, and long-term strategic initiatives across the global engineering organization. This leader will partner closely with the EVP of Engineering, and other cross-functional executives to align engineering processes, resources, and roadmaps with the company’s strategic goals.

This role serves as a critical link between engineering execution and business strategy, ensuring teams operate with efficiency, deliver high-quality outcomes, and align with broader company objectives.

We are seeking a seasoned Engineering Director to lead the growth and scale of engineering operations programs. As part of the Engineering Strategy & Operations team, this role is responsible for building a world-class technical enablement and deployment program that empowers engineers to tackle complex customer challenges using the Salesforce Platform.

The ideal candidate is a data-driven, people-centric leader who brings a blend of strategic thinking, operational discipline, and deep understanding of modern engineering practices.

You will be responsible for defining onboarding experiences, technical training programs, and engagement models that prepare engineers to deliver secure, scalable, and high-performance solutions. Your efforts will lay the groundwork for how engineers drive platform adoption, enhance technical health, and accelerate customer time-to-value in the field.

This is a hands-on leadership position that blends strategic program ownership, technical acumen, and operational scale. You will collaborate closely with cross-functional partners across Product, Engineering, and Customer Success to instill engineering rigor and deliver impactful solutions across Marketing and Salesforce Clouds.

Your Impact

Engineering Leadership & Enablement

Lead and grow a team of technical enablement leads and program managers responsible for onboarding and scaling engineers globally.

Foster a culture of engineering excellence, continuous learning, and customer-first problem solving.

Provide technical mentorship and architectural guidance to new and existing engineers.

Build pathways for engineers to progress from onboarding to technical leadership in the field.

Program & Technical Strategy

Define the technical vision for the engineering program, ensuring alignment with Salesforce platform best practices, guardrails, and product roadmaps.

Build scalable onboarding, training, and certification programs to equip engineers with deep Salesforce technical expertise across the platform, Revenue Cloud, and Industries solutions.

Develop and scale technical engagement models (e.g., Implementation, MVP, Advisory) for repeatable, high-impact customer engagements.

Drive operational frameworks for knowledge sharing, reusable code assets, and engagement playbooks across the engineering community.

Evaluate program outcomes using data-driven insights to guide decision-making and refine future strategies.

Cross-Functional Collaboration

Work closely with Salesforce Product and Engineering teams to align field feedback with product improvements.

Partner with Customer Success and Professional Services to define technical handoffs and ensure long-term customer success.

Serve as the technical escalation point for complex deployments and customer technical blockers.

Requirements

15+ years of engineering leadership experience in SaaS or enterprise software environments. roles, including 8+ years in leadership.

Experience in scaling engineering teams and operations in fast-paced environments with background in change management and scaling operational frameworks.

Proven track record managing cross-functional, globally distributed engineering teams and large-scale program

Deep technical expertise in Salesforce Platform architecture, application development, and scale/performance best practices

Strong understanding of enterprise SaaS deployment models, platform guardrails, and technical health frameworks.

Strong understanding of software development lifecycle (SDLC), DevOps, Agile, CI/CD 

Operational mindset with experience scaling global programs and managing technical processes.

Excellent communication and leadership skills with the ability to influence senior engineers, product teams, and customer stakeholders.

Education 

Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field 

In office expectations are 10 days/a quarter to support customers and/or collaborate with their teams.

Relocation is not provided for this opportunity.

Application Deadline: March 11, 2026.

Role Overview:
As an SDR Apprentice at Salesforce, you aren’t just starting a career in sales. You are joining the #1 AI CRM to redefine how business is done. This role is a unique hybrid of sales development and operational efficiency. You will be the operational excellence of our SMB (Small & Medium Business) team, leveraging cutting-edge AI tools to streamline workflows while building the foundational skills to become a world-class Sales Development Representative.

The Salesforce SDR Apprentice Program is a fast-track, high-impact initiative. You will work at the intersection of human creativity and Agentic AI, learning to use our Customer 360 platform to identify opportunities, optimize lead flows, and drive growth for our customers.

Career Acceleration: Successful completion of this 3-5 month program positions you to advance to the Sales Development Representative role, equipped with a technical and operational edge that sets you apart in the industry.

Key Responsibilities:

Operational Efficiency: Streamline internal workflows and business processes, leveraging AI-powered insights to ensure the SDR organization operates at peak productivity.

Pipeline Development: Qualify inbound leads from marketing campaigns and events using the Customer 360 platform and AI tools to prioritize high-potential SMB opportunities.

CRM Management: Orchestrate prospecting activity and lead conversion within Salesforce, ensuring data integrity fuels our AI models and sales forecasting.

Strategic Research: Understand intent signals of target accounts using Salesforce’s AI-driven intelligence to identify key stakeholders and craft data-backed outreach strategies.

Qualifications:

Sales Driven: A clear desire to build a long term career in professional sales and a passion for mastering the #1 AI CRM. Previous internship or work experience in sales is a plus.

Resilient and Goal Oriented: A high level of accountability toward hitting targets and the resilience to remain positive through the challenges of high volume outreach.

Operational Problem Solver: A natural ability to break down complex challenges and develop efficient step by step technical solutions.

Effective Communicator: Strong verbal and written skills with the ability to convey complex value propositions clearly and professionally.

Growth Mindset: A self motivated learner who is eager to master the tools and skills required to progress into a full cycle sales role.

Why Salesforce:

Hands-on Experience: Gain real-world experience in lead qualification and customer profiling while serving as the operational backbone of the team. You will balance direct prospect engagement with critical business operations, ensuring our systems run at peak efficiency while you develop the core sales skills needed for your next career step.

Growth Trajectory: Build your foundation on the #1 AI CRM. You will develop the technical and operational fluency that serves as a launchpad for your career at Salesforce. This role provides the blueprint to grow into a high-impact sales career.

Community: Join a team built on Trust and Innovation. We prioritize continuous mentorship and collaborative problem-solving, ensuring you have the support to bring your full self to work while making a tangible impact on how businesses grow.

We're Salesforce, the Customer Company. If you believe in bringing companies and customers together, in business as the greatest platform for change, in creating a more equitable and sustainable future for all – well, you're in the right place. Through our #1 CRM, Customer 360, we help companies blaze new trails and connect with their customers in a whole new way. And, we empower you to be a Trailblazer, too — driving your performance and growth, charting new paths, and improving the state of the world.

What you'll be doing...

As an Enterprise Account Executive at MuleSoft, you will own the full sales cycle for strategic enterprise accounts, driving the adoption of MuleSoft's Anypoint Platform — the world's leading integration and API management solution. You will partner with C-level executives to help them unlock the full value of their digital transformation initiatives by connecting applications, data, and devices across any cloud or on-premises environment.

Drive net-new revenue and expand existing accounts by selling MuleSoft's Anypoint Platform to enterprise customers across a defined territory.

Develop and execute a strategic territory plan to achieve and exceed annual quota targets.

Lead complex, multi-stakeholder sales cycles from discovery through close, engaging executive sponsors and navigating enterprise procurement processes.

Build and maintain trusted relationships with C-level and senior IT and business leaders, positioning MuleSoft as a strategic platform for integration, API management, and digital transformation.

Forecast accurately and manage pipeline with rigor, using Salesforce CRM as your system of record.

Collaborate cross-functionally with Solution Engineers, Customer Success, Professional Services, and MuleSoft/Salesforce product teams to deliver exceptional customer outcomes.

Stay current on MuleSoft's product roadmap, competitive landscape, and industry trends to effectively position our platform value.

Champion customer advocacy by building a base of referenceable, satisfied enterprise clients.

Who you are...

8+ years of enterprise software sales experience, with a proven track record of exceeding quota selling complex, solution-based technology.

Experience selling integration, middleware, API management, or cloud platform solutions is strongly preferred.

Demonstrated success managing long, complex sales cycles with multiple stakeholders at the C-suite level.

Strong consultative selling skills with the ability to connect technical solutions to business outcomes.

Proven ability to build and execute strategic territory and account plans.

Highly collaborative with the ability to orchestrate internal resources across a matrixed organization.

Strong analytical and operational skills; comfortable with pipeline management, forecasting, and business reviews.

Experience working in or selling to industries such as Financial Services, Healthcare, Retail, or Manufacturing is a plus.

Role Overview:
As an SDR Apprentice at Salesforce, you aren’t just starting a career in sales. You are joining the #1 AI CRM to redefine how business is done. This role is a unique hybrid of sales development and operational efficiency. You will be the operational excellence of our SMB (Small & Medium Business) team, leveraging cutting-edge AI tools to streamline workflows while building the foundational skills to become a world-class Sales Development Representative.

The Salesforce SDR Apprentice Program is a fast-track, high-impact initiative. You will work at the intersection of human creativity and Agentic AI, learning to use our Customer 360 platform to identify opportunities, optimize lead flows, and drive growth for our customers.

You will balance direct prospect engagement with critical business operations, ensuring our systems run at peak efficiency while you develop the core sales skills needed for your next career step.

À propos de MuleSoft

L'expérience et les attentes des clients n'ont jamais été aussi déterminantes. L'intelligence artificielle permet désormais aux organisations d'innover plus rapidement, offrant à chaque collaborateur en contact avec la clientèle les moyens de créer des expériences connectées. Bien que l'IA générative soit une technologie puissante, sa pertinence dépend intrinsèquement de la qualité des données qui l'alimentent.

Grâce aux solutions d'intégration, d'automatisation et de gestion universelle d'API de MuleSoft, nos clients peuvent connecter et exploiter les données issues de n'importe quelle source de manière efficace et rapide. L'intégration constitue le fondement d'une IA actionnable et, avec MuleSoft, les agents Agentforce peuvent intervenir en tout point de l'organisation. Intégrez n'importe quel système de manière fluide, orchestrez des flux de travail complexes et sécurisez les API critiques afin d'étendre la puissance d'Agentforce à l'ensemble de l'environnement informatique, créant ainsi des expériences d'exception pour les collaborateurs comme pour les clients.

Description du poste

Salesforce recherche des talents d'exception pour rejoindre nos équipes en tant qu'Ingénieur Commercial / Account Executive, dotés d'un solide bagage technique et d'une expertise confirmée dans les solutions basées sur l'IA. Dans ce rôle, vous jouerez un rôle pivot afin de stimuler la croissance du chiffre d'affaires, de pérenniser les relations clients et de déployer des technologies innovantes qui redéfinissent l'engagement client.

Il ne s'agit pas d'un rôle commercial tactique conventionnel. Vous serez un membre clé d'une équipe pluridisciplinaire soudée, responsable de la définition et de l'exécution de la stratégie de mise sur le marché (Go-to-Market) de votre secteur, ainsi que du pilotage de l'intégralité du cycle de vente. Nous ouvrons la voie non seulement pour transformer la manière dont nos clients conçoivent leurs logiciels, mais également pour révolutionner leur modèle opérationnel. Vous valoriserez des résultats stratégiques à travers des engagements de croissance à long terme.

Ce poste constitue un défi stimulant pour votre évolution personnelle et professionnelle : la majorité de nos dirigeants sur le terrain sont issus de promotions internes, et vous évoluerez aux côtés d'experts de renommée mondiale qui sauront vous challenger et vous soutenir pour réaliser les plus grands accomplissements de votre carrière.

Missions et responsabilités

Identifier, qualifier et conclure proactivement des opportunités de vente afin de développer le pipeline.

Mener une prospection stratégique auprès des directions techniques et des systèmes d'information (CTO/DSI), des responsables ingénierie et des dirigeants d'entreprise.

Établir des relations professionnelles solides et pérennes, génératrices d'opportunités de croissance.

Collaborer avec nos Ingénieurs Avant-Vente (Solution Engineers) et travailler en étroite synergie avec l'équipe Professional Services pour garantir la satisfaction client.

Œuvrer de concert avec les partenaires de l'écosystème et les partenaires de distribution afin d'optimiser l'envergure des contrats.

Participer activement à nos programmes de Sales Enablement, à nos formations de vente avancées ainsi qu'à nos programmes de leadership et de développement.

Profil recherché

Expérience récente et probante démontrant votre succès dans la gestion de ventes complexes et à fort enjeu commercial auprès de décideurs IT et métiers.

Historique avéré de dépassement d'objectifs dans des fonctions de vente de logiciels avec quotas.

Expertise dans la conquête de nouveaux comptes (New Business).

Enthousiasme pour le développement de secteurs à fort potentiel (Greenfield) et pour la construction d'une activité commerciale à partir de zéro.

Engagement marqué envers la réussite client, fondé sur une approche de vente consultative et axée sur les résultats.

Aptitude à communiquer avec franchise et intégrité, en agissant toujours dans l'intérêt de vos interlocuteurs.

Esprit d'équipe collaboratif avec une mentalité axée sur les intérêts de l'entreprise — la bienveillance est l'une de nos valeurs fondamentales : nous privilégions le succès collectif et l'entraide pour atteindre des résultats d'excellence.

Une connaissance de la gestion d'API et de l'intégration est un atout, sans être une exigence absolue.

Avantages et bénéfices

Nous vous invitons à consulter notre portail dédié aux avantages sociaux, qui détaille nos divers programmes, notamment le remboursement des frais de bien-être, un congé parental généreux, l'aide à l'adoption, les prestations liées à la fertilité, et bien d'autres mesures de soutien.

Aménagements

Engagement de l'employeur

Chez Salesforce, nous sommes convaincus que le rôle de l'entreprise est d'améliorer l'état de notre monde. Chacun d'entre nous a la responsabilité de favoriser l'égalité dans nos communautés et sur nos lieux de travail. Nous nous engageons à créer un effectif qui reflète la société à travers des programmes et des initiatives inclusives, tels que l'égalité salariale, les groupes de ressources pour les employés, des avantages inclusifs, et bien plus encore. Découvrez-en davantage sur l'Égalité chez Salesforce et explorez nos avantages.

Salesforce est un employeur garantissant l'égalité des chances et l'action positive. About MuleSoft

Customer experience and expectations have never been more decisive. Artificial intelligence now enables organizations to innovate faster, providing every customer-facing employee with the means to create connected experiences. While generative AI is a powerful technology, its relevance inherently depends on the quality of the data that fuels it.

Through MuleSoft’s integration, automation, and universal API management solutions, our customers can connect and leverage data from any source efficiently and quickly. Integration forms the foundation of actionable AI, and with MuleSoft, Agentforce agents can intervene at any point in the organization. Seamlessly integrate any system, orchestrate complex workflows, and secure critical APIs to extend the power of Agentforce across the entire IT environment, creating exceptional experiences for both employees and customers.

Job Description

Salesforce is seeking exceptional talent to join our teams as an Account Executive, equipped with a strong technical background and proven expertise in AI-based solutions. In this role, you will play a pivotal part in driving revenue growth, sustaining customer relationships, and deploying innovative technologies that redefine customer engagement.

This is not a conventional tactical sales role. You will be a key member of a close-knit, multidisciplinary team responsible for defining and executing the Go-to-Market (GTM) strategy for your sector, as well as managing the entire sales cycle. We are leading the way not only in transforming how our customers build software, but also in revolutionizing their operating models. You will drive strategic results through long-term growth engagements.

This position offers a stimulating challenge for your personal and professional growth: the majority of our field leaders are internal promotions, and you will work alongside world-renowned experts who will challenge and support you in achieving the greatest accomplishments of your career.

Missions and Responsibilities

Proactively identify, qualify, and close sales opportunities to develop the pipeline.

Conduct strategic prospecting with technical and information systems leadership (CTO/CIO), engineering leads, and business executives.

Establish strong, lasting professional relationships that generate growth opportunities.

Collaborate with our Solution Engineers (Pre-sales) and work in close synergy with the Professional Services team to guarantee customer satisfaction.

Work in concert with ecosystem and distribution partners to optimize deal size and scope.

Actively participate in our Sales Enablement programs, advanced sales training, and our leadership and development initiatives.

Required Profile

Recent and successful experience demonstrating your ability to manage complex, high-stakes sales with both IT and business decision-makers.

A proven track record of exceeding targets in quota-carrying software sales roles.

Expertise in New Business acquisition.

Enthusiasm for developing high-potential sectors (Greenfield) and building a sales business from the ground up.

A strong commitment to customer success, based on a consultative and results-oriented sales approach.

Ability to communicate with candor and integrity, always acting in the best interest of your stakeholders.

A collaborative team spirit with a "company-first" mentality—kindness is one of our core values: we prioritize collective success and mutual support to achieve excellence.

Knowledge of API management and integration is an asset, but not an absolute requirement.

Unleash Your Potential

When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Overview of the Role:

The Data Analytics Manager will be a crucial member of a high-performance team, partnering closely with Renewals, Sales Strategy, Finance, Product, and Field Leadership to support strategies that improve retention. This role blends expertise in data analytics, predictive modeling, renewals strategy, and business operations. Success in this role means supporting the development of the Attrition Insight Center of Excellence (CoE), the source of truth for renewals and attrition data at Salesforce. 

Sample deliverables range from the strategic to the operational including building scalable attrition insights dashboards, running deep dive analyses, creating and presenting enablement materials and developing detailed analytic models. 

Responsibilities:

Partner with Renewals, Sales Strategy, Finance, Product, and Field Leadership to proactively identify attrition drivers and mitigation strategies

Collaborate with Data Engineering to enhance data infrastructure and data governance

Conduct deep dive analyses on attrition leveraging large volumes of datasets using SQL, Tableau, Python, etc. 

Document processes, data sources, metric definitions and enablement materials for diverse stakeholders

Build and deliver presentations with recommendations to senior executives

Develop self service reporting through dashboards, toolkits and 

Support automation and enhancement of key processes to improve accuracy, timeliness and scalability of insights delivered

Desired Skills and Experience:

7+ years of business analytics experience

High level of confidence and comfort in working autonomously without constant supervision (self-starter, strong initiative, fast learner)

Strong communication skills, both written and verbal

Degree in a quantitative field or equivalent relevant experience required.

Proficiency with SQL(Snowflake) and Tableau. Python and/or R a plus

Experience creating BI dashboards and assets end-to-end; from initial scoping, data wrangling, asset creation and deployment

Strong presentation skills

Use of AI in daily operations

Great teammate able to lead and work effectively at all levels of an organization with the ability to influence others to move toward consensus

Strong situational analysis, negotiation, and decision-making abilities.

Detail and quality-oriented with the desire to quickly learn new concepts, business models, and technologies

About Salesforce

Salesforce is the #1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. We are looking for a Lead Penetration Testing Engineer to execute deep, high-impact penetration testing across our applications, platforms, cloud infrastructure, and enterprise environments, including AI-powered features and systems.

This role is highly technical, hands-on, with a strong focus on real exploitation, attack chaining, risk impact, and with a hacker mindset, rather than checklist-driven testing. You will lead complex engagements end to end, ensuring findings are translated into concrete engineering improvements and mitigation.

In addition to execution, you will help shape the penetration testing strategy, scope engagements effectively, mentor other testers, and act as a trusted technical partner to engineering and security leadership.

Key Responsibilities

Lead and execute advanced penetration tests across:

Web applications and APIs
 

Cloud and hybrid infrastructure (k8, docker etc)
 

Identity, authorization, and trust boundaries
 

Internal and external enterprise attack surfaces
 

AI / ML-enabled systems (e.g., LLM-backed applications, Agentic AI)
 

Identify, exploit and demonstrate realistic business and risk impact 

Perform advanced penetration testing activities, including:

Manual exploitation beyond automated tooling
 

Business logic and authorization abuse
 

Privilege escalation
 

Abuse of identity, access, and trust relationships
 

AI-specific offensive testing, including prompt injection, indirect prompt injection, and abuse of AI integrations
 

Own penetration testing engagements end-to-end via:

Scoping
 

Test execution
 

Risk assessment and prioritization
 

Clear reporting and remediation guidance
 

Develop deep technical understanding of systems and products to uncover systemic weaknesses, not just isolated bugs, including weaknesses introduced by AI-driven components.

Partner closely with:

Engineering teams to explain root causes and exploitation paths
 

Security architects and AppSec teams to influence design and guardrails
 

Detection & Response teams where findings have monitoring or alerting implications
 

Produce high-quality, technically detailed reports that clearly explain Exploitation path, missing /lacking Security Controls and Mitigation solutions

Contribute to tooling, automation, and testing frameworks where it improves scale or consistency (without replacing deep manual testing).

Required Qualifications

Deep hands-on experience in penetration testing, offensive security, or application security testing.

5+ years of experience in penetration testing, offensive security, and vulnerability research.

Proven experience leading complex penetration testing engagements in production or production-like environments.

Strong understanding of:

Application security vulnerabilities and attack chains
 

Identity and access control failures
 

Cloud security and hybrid environments
 

Common defensive controls and their real-world limitations
 

Security risks specific to AI and LLM-based systems
 

Hands-on experience with:

Manual exploitation and vulnerability chaining
 

Custom scripts, payloads, or proof-of-concept development
 

Advanced use (and limitation awareness) of automated testing tools
 

Testing AI-powered applications and APIs
 

Ability to clearly articulate:

Exploitation mechanics and impact
 

Risk in business and engineering terms
 

Practical, prioritized remediation strategies
 

Strong communication skills and experience working directly with engineers, security teams, and leadership.

About Salesforce

Salesforce is the #1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all.

Ready to level-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce.

Role Overview

Join our world-class Customer Success Employee Success Business Partner (ESBP) team. This Senior Director-level role supports account-aligned organizations within Salesforce and partners directly with senior Account Success leaders driving long-term vision and strategy for our most high-impact businesses.

We are seeking a strategic HR/ESBP leader who embodies our culture and values, with a strong track record of influencing executive stakeholders and delivering measurable business results in complex, fast-paced technology environments.

In this role, you will align people strategy with business objectives, enabling growth, innovation, and organizational effectiveness across a large-scale portfolio.

Key Responsibilities

Executive Partnership & Strategic Advising

Serve as a trusted advisor to senior leaders.

Influence business and talent priorities through strong executive relationships and professional credibility.

Partner with leadership teams to understand business goals, product roadmaps, and technical strategies that impact workforce planning and organization design.

Workforce Strategy & Organizational Design

Proactively advise on workforce planning, location strategy, organizational structure, skills evolution, and long-term capability building aligned to cloud growth objectives.

Lead organizational design efforts in high-growth and transformation environments.

Act as a change agent as the organization evolves in response to market dynamics, competitive pressures, AI-driven innovation, and technological advancement.

Talent Strategy & Leadership Development

Lead and execute end-to-end talent planning processes, including succession planning, leadership development, and high-potential identification.

Assess leadership capability and accelerate development within highly technical, product-driven environments.

Drive employee engagement and retention strategies in partnership with leadership.

Equality, Inclusion & People Programs

Partner to advance Equality initiatives, including representation strategy, talent movement, recruiting alignment, and targeted development efforts.

Collaborate with Centers of Excellence (CoEs) and global HR/ES teams to design and implement scalable people initiatives.

Ensure seamless execution of employee success processes and programs.

Data-Driven Decision Making

Analyze business trends, product strategy shifts, and talent metrics to inform proactive, data-driven people strategies.

Translate insights into actionable solutions that enhance performance and organizational effectiveness.

Key Qualifications

Demonstrated change management expertise within fast-paced technology organizations.

Deep HR/ES business partner experience diagnosing complex organizational challenges and mobilizing effective solutions.

Strong talent assessment and leadership development capabilities in engineering and product environments.

High business acumen with the ability to connect talent strategy to financial and operational outcomes.

Proven ability to build trusted partnerships with senior leaders.

Results-oriented with a strong drive to create meaningful organizational impact.

Education & Experience

8+ years of HR/ES business partner experience.

Bachelor’s degree required.

Advanced degree in Human Resources, Organization Development, Business, or related field strongly preferred.

The Customer Success M&A Lead, will be responsible for driving the successful onboarding and integration of acquired companies into the Customer Success organization. This role will manage a portfolio of acquisitions, ensuring a seamless and effective transition of people, processes, and systems. The M&A Lead will be accountable for driving business continuity during onboarding and defining the integration strategy, aligning business value, and executing on the integration roadmap to accelerate acquired companies' productivity and performance.

This is a highly strategic and collaborative role that requires a blend of critical thinking, excellent execution, and strong stakeholder management. The role is part of the Customer Success M&A team, focused on developing and executing rapid and thoughtful integrations that drive customer adoption and reduce attrition. The successful candidate will build trust with leaders across acquired companies and the broader Salesforce organization to drive alignment on integration priorities and roadmaps while consistently living the organization's core values.

Responsibilities:

Stakeholder Engagement & Management: Build and maintain effective and trusted relationships with internal teams and acquired company stakeholders at all levels. Ensure executive alignment on the integration program, priorities, and roadmap to drive successful outcomes.

Strategic Narrative & Value Articulation: Translate complex M&A strategies, decisions, and challenges into clear, compelling narratives and communications. Articulate the program's value throughout the M&A lifecycle to manage change and drive the program towards completion.

Program Leadership & Execution: Define and validate post-announce integration strategy, lead execution planning, and manage resources and timelines for a portfolio of acquisitions. Drive the achievement of V2MOM and program success metrics across the M&A integration lifecycle.

Cross-Functional Collaboration: Collaborate with a wide range of cross-functional teams, including Corporate Development, Business Technology, Finance, and Field Leadership, to ensure alignment and encourage collaboration throughout the integration journey.

Influence and Advise: Proactively identify trends, risks, and opportunities to influence and advise the strategy. Highlight key issues to senior leadership, partnering with them to solve problems and make informed decisions.

Continuous Improvement: Drive continuous learning and organizational excellence in M&A, refining integration frameworks and best practices to enable sustainable and scalable transformations.

Required Experience & Skills:

6-8 years of relevant work experience in strategy consulting, management consulting, M&A integration management, or a related field, with a proven track record of success.

Proven experience in Program Leadership & Execution for large tech acquisitions, demonstrating a deep understanding of the integration process and best practices.

Demonstrated ability to think strategically while being meticulous, considering the broader business context and implications.

Exceptional Stakeholder Engagement & Management skills, with a proven ability to build strong relationships, navigate complex organizational dynamics, and drive alignment at all levels.

Strong strategic and critical thinking skills with a passion for developing solutions to complex business problems.

Excellent communication skills, with a track record in crafting visually appealing and compelling stories and recommendations for executive-level consumption. Proven ability to translate complex strategies into clear, compelling narratives that drive understanding and action (Strategic Narrative & Value Articulation).

Strong business acumen and analytical approach, with the ability to analyze data, identify trends, and leverage insights to advise integration strategies.

Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, dynamic environment and manage multiple projects simultaneously, prioritizing tasks, and delivering high-quality results within established timelines.

A track record of successfully demonstrating the organization's core values while navigating M&A complexities, fostering trust, and leading an equitable integration process (Living Our Values).

The Agentic Onboarding and Learning Delivery team is seeking a passionate and detail-oriented intern to support the delivery of world-class onboarding and learning experiences for employees across Latin America and globally. In this role, you'll gain hands-on experience in learning design, program coordination, and employee enablement while working alongside experienced learning professionals.

Key Responsibilities:

Support the coordination and delivery of onboarding programs for new hires in the LATAM region, as well as virtual support globally

Assist in the development and maintenance of learning materials, including presentations, guides, and digital content

Coordinate logistics for onboarding and talent experience events, including scheduling, communications, and participant tracking

Gather feedback from participants and analyze data to help improve learning programs

Help facilitate virtual and in-person learning sessions and workshops

Support the team with administrative tasks such as updating learning management systems, deck presentations, creating reports, and managing calendars

Collaborate with cross-functional teams including HR, Talent Acquisition, and business leaders

Bachelor's degree with graduation date between June 2027 and December 2027

English advanced

Strong organizational skills and exceptional attention to detail

Passion for learning, development, and employee experience

Ability to work collaboratively in a team environment

Self-motivated with a proactive approach to problem-solving

Creative mindset with an eye for design and content development

Previous internship or work experience in HR, training, or education is a plus

Previous experience coordinating events or programs is a plus

What We Offer:

The opportunity to learn from industry leaders and grow your career in a Fortune 500 company.

A culture of continuous learning and development, including mentorship and formal training programs.

Competitive salary and benefits, including health, and vision coverage, along with employee wellness programs.

A chance to make an impact by helping businesses grow through innovative Salesforce solutions.

Part-time.

Job Details

About Salesforce

The Event Technology team delivers a cutting-edge technology stack that enhances in-person, digital, and on-demand experiences across Salesforce’s event portfolio. Our industry-leading platforms and solutions help connect target audiences to the content they need, when and where they need it so that it is as easy as possible for them to learn how to use Salesforce products and drive business growth.

Salesforce delivers world-class events, both in-person and virtually. As the Principal Member of Technical Staff focused on architecture priorities for the Event Technology portfolio, you will play a crucial role in defining and driving the technical vision and strategy. You will oversee the architectural integrity of our platforms and ensure they align with our future state objectives. This leadership role requires a deep understanding of software development and architecture principles and a passion for innovation and excellence in scalability, performance, observability and user experience.

In addition to Events Technology, you will play a part in other public web experiences including www.tableau.com, www.mulesoft.com, and “role based” sites such as the developer, admin and architect subdomains of Salesforce.com.

“You build it, you own it.” Our engineering teams are responsible for the ongoing maintenance of our apps. Therefore this role will at times require active engagement on deployments, monitoring, incident management and response, vendor oversight, and general ongoing operations activities.

Key Responsibilities:

Lead the architectural strategy and direction for the Event Technology and websites portfolio, ensuring alignment with Marketing Technology’s overall technology vision and business goals.

Architect, design, implement, test, and deliver highly scalable applications and services.

Operate optimally in the hybrid engineering model where engineers are encouraged to craft and complete the vital work to ensure quality in their code and other engineers.

Strategically plan, design, and execute the implementation of highly scalable solutions that meet current and future business needs.

Collaborate with Architects, Lead Engineers, Product, UX and cross-functional teams in the application design process, contributing innovative ideas and technical expertise.

Required Skills:

10+ years of professional software development experience in designing, building, scaling, and maintaining production systems.

Proven experience in a technical leadership role, including architecture, design, and implementation of complex software systems.

Strong understanding and embodiment of service ownership principles and skills; expertise in building observable and resilient systems with the ability to proactively prevent issues before our customers even notice.

Experience with domain driven design.

Proven ability to mentor team members to support their understanding and growth of software engineering concepts and aid in their technical development.

Experience developing front-end and back-end software, preferably including JavaScript with TypeScript, with frameworks such as React, runtimes including Node.js and CSS frameworks such as Tailwind or Sass.

Experience building high-scale microservices on AWS (preferred), GCP, or other public cloud substrates. Examples of AWS services we use include Lambda, DynamoDB, SNS, SQS, EventBridge, API Gateway, and more.

Expertise in consuming (and ideally building) GraphQL and RESTful APIs and strong understanding of API security best practices.

Proven experience leveraging AI tools (Claude, Cursor, etc.) in your daily workflows and the ability to uplevel other engineers’ usage of AI tools.

Preferred Skills:

Knowledge of mobile and cross-platform/browser test automation.

Experience with Snowflake, Google Analytics, and configuring CDNs such as Akamai.

Proven ability to collaborate closely with cross-functional teams, including product managers, designers, and other engineering teams, to deliver exceptional user experiences.

Strong understanding of Agile methodologies: Scrum and Kanban.

Bachelor's or Master's in Computer Science, Technology, or equivalent experience.

Job Details

About Salesforce

Salesforce is the #1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all.

THE ROLE

Marketing Cloud Customer Onboarding Specialists warmly welcome our newest Salesforce marketing cloud customers post-sale and guide them through the critical onboarding phase before implementation begins. This role is designed for experienced professionals who act as trusted advisors, ensuring customers achieve rapid time to value while building a strong foundation for long-term success across Salesforce products—particularly Marketing Cloud and the Salesforce Platform.

You will act as a trusted advisor during early customer engagements, leading strategic onboarding conversations including but not limited to - Marketing Cloud Engagement, Marketing Cloud Account Engagement (Pardot), Marketing Cloud Personalization, Marketing Cloud Intelligence (Datorama), Marketing Cloud Growth, Advanced Editions, and Third-Party Messaging, ensuring customers are prepared for successful implementation and adoption.
 

RESPONSIBILITIES

Serve as a senior subject-matter expert for Salesforce marketing cloud products, with a strong focus on accelerating initial customer value.

Act as the first post-sale point of contact, providing a confident, warm, and strategic welcome into the Salesforce ecosystem.

Lead virtual 1:1 and group onboarding engagements, educating customers on how to realize business value using Salesforce Customer 360.

Apply consultative skills to uncover business objectives, use cases, and success metrics, shaping long-term customer success.

Design and deliver tailored Success Paths, outlining clear steps to meet customer goals.

Provide strategic, value-based recommendations aligned to customer industry, maturity, and growth plans.

Build strong relationships with customers and internal partners, contributing to regional and global onboarding excellence.

Actively contribute to and elevate the team’s knowledge base, best practices, and onboarding standards.

Mentor and support junior onboarding specialists as a senior team member along with participating in organisation wide workstreams.

REQUIRED EXPERIENCE

Proven success delivering customer-facing presentations and strategic onboarding engagements.

Relevant third-level degree or equivalent professional experience.

4+ years of experience helping customers achieve business outcomes through technology.

Strong ability to work independently, manage priorities, and perform effectively in a fast-paced environment.

Demonstrated application of Customer Success frameworks and methodologies.

Confidence engaging with stakeholders from Administrators to C-suite.

REQUIRED SKILLS & QUALITICATIONS

Fluency in English

Strong business acumen—ability to connect Salesforce capabilities to customer business value.

Exceptional communication and presentation skills, adaptable to varied audiences.

Proven consultative engagement experience with enterprise or mid-market customers.

Relationship-driven, personable, and customer-centric approach.

Strong technical aptitude and ability to learn new Salesforce technologies quickly.

Effective problem-solving and decision-making skills.

Comfort operating in environments of continuous change.

PREFERRED REQUIREMENTS

Salesforce Certifications (Preferred): Marketing Cloud Administrator - Marketing Cloud Email Specialist - Marketing Cloud Consultant - Salesforce Administrator - Agentforce Specialist

Hands-on experience with Salesforce Marketing Cloud and CRM platforms.

Understanding of software implementation and onboarding strategies.

Knowledge of project management principles.

Strong data literacy, including reporting and insight interpretation.

Ability to prioritize effectively under pressure.

Experience will be evaluated based on alignment to the core proficiencies for the role (e.g. extracurricular leadership roles, volunteer work, etc.).

NOTE: By applying to the Sr Success Guide posting recruiters and hiring managers across the organization hiring these roles will review your resume. Our goal is for you to apply once and have your resume reviewed by multiple hiring teams.

BENEFITS & PERKS

Comprehensive benefits package including well-being reimbursement, generous parental leave, adoption assistance, fertility benefits, and more!

World-class enablement and on-demand training with Trailhead.com

Exposure to executive thought leaders and regular 1:1 coaching with leadership

Volunteer opportunities and participation in our 1:1:1 model for giving back to the community

For more details, visit https://www.salesforcebenefits.com/

We are seeking a dynamic and experienced Senior Manager of Sales Onboarding and Leadership Development to lead and enhance our global sales onboarding programs and leadership development initiatives. This role requires a strategic leader with strong operational expertise to manage logistical coordination, oversee LMS path management, analyze ramp data, facilitate live training sessions, provide coaching, and collaborate closely with cross-functional teams across AMER, APJ, and EMEA regions.

Key Responsibilities:

Sales Onboarding Program Management: Design, implement, and continuously improve comprehensive onboarding programs for new sales hires to accelerate productivity and engagement.

Logistical Coordination: Manage all logistical aspects of onboarding and leadership development programs, including scheduling, resource allocation, and communication across multiple time zones.

LMS Path Management: Oversee the learning management system (LMS) to ensure sales onboarding and leadership development content is current, accessible, and aligned with business goals. Develop and maintain learning paths tailored to different roles and regions.

Ramp Data & Analytics: Track, analyze, and report on ramp metrics and program effectiveness to identify trends, gaps, and opportunities for improvement. Use data to inform program adjustments and leadership decisions.

Live Session Facilitation: Lead and facilitate engaging live training sessions, workshops, and webinars for sales teams and leaders globally, ensuring content is relevant and impactful.

Coaching & Development: Provide one-on-one and group coaching to sales leaders and new hires to support skill development, performance improvement, and leadership growth.

Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner with sales leadership, HR, enablement teams, and regional stakeholders to align onboarding and leadership development initiatives with global business objectives and cultural nuances.

Global Impact: Drive consistency and scalability of programs across AMER, APJ, and EMEA, adapting strategies to meet regional needs while maintaining a unified global approach.

Strategy & Expansion:

Develop and execute a scalable onboarding and leadership development strategy that supports rapid growth and expansion into new markets and geographies.

Identify opportunities to standardize core program elements while allowing for regional customization to respect cultural and market differences.

Collaborate with business leaders to forecast talent needs and align onboarding capacity and leadership development pipelines accordingly.

Leverage technology and automation to streamline program delivery and improve learner engagement and tracking.

Continuously assess emerging trends in sales enablement, adult learning, and leadership development to innovate and keep programs cutting-edge.

Build a roadmap for expanding leadership development initiatives beyond sales to include cross-functional leadership growth opportunities.

Establish metrics and KPIs to measure the success of expansion efforts and adjust strategies based on data-driven insights.

Additional Considerations for the Role:

Ensure compliance with regional regulations and data privacy laws related to learning and development activities.

Foster an inclusive learning environment that supports diversity and equity across all regions.

Manage vendor relationships and partnerships that support content development, technology platforms, and training delivery.

Champion a feedback culture by soliciting input from participants and stakeholders to continuously improve program quality.

Balance strategic planning with hands-on execution to lead by example and inspire the team.

Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in Business, Human Resources, Education, or related field; advanced degree preferred.

5-7+ years of experience in sales enablement, onboarding, leadership development, or related fields

Proven experience managing LMS platforms and developing structured learning paths.

Strong analytical skills with experience in ramp data analysis and reporting.

Excellent facilitation and presentation skills with a track record of delivering live training sessions.

Demonstrated coaching ability with a focus on leadership and sales performance.

Exceptional organizational and project management skills, capable of managing complex logistics across multiple regions.

Experience working in or supporting global teams, with knowledge of AMER, APJ, and EMEA markets.

Strong interpersonal and collaboration skills to work effectively with diverse teams and stakeholders.

We are seeking a U.S. based Employee Success People Consultant (ESPC) to join our ES organization. This role will have significant scope and impact, partnering closely with ES teams including ESBPs, COEs, People Services & Operations (PS&O), Reward Partners and more to coordinate, roll out and complete ES programs and initiatives that support the business needs of the organization. This role will provide dedicated support to ESBPs and Leaders within the sales distribution team on org health reviews, org changes, promotions, talent reviews, performance reviews, employee experience surveys, and more. The ideal candidate is one who excels in a fast-paced and everchanging environment, is strategic and proactive, and is looking to work with a strong ES Organization.

Responsibilities:

Act as a trusted advisor to ESBP Partners and business leaders, ensuring HR strategies support business needs.

Provide operational and analytical support to the ESBPs with quantitative and qualitative data in support of strategic ES programs and initiatives.

Monitor and interpret trends and metrics around employee satisfaction, attrition and turnover, etc. Use HR analytic tools to make data-driven business decisions.

Proactively understand what analysis needs to be done to provide a narrative to leaders on their overall org health and propose relevant ES activities.

Support digital transformation efforts through use of AI, Agents, UAT and continuous improvement of our processes.

Partner with ESBPs, Centers of Excellence (COEs) and global partners to help deliver seamless human resources processes, programs and insights across the organization.

Partner with the ESBPs and the business units they directly support to deliver on the organization's talent strategy. Initiatives include employee retention, talent assessment, succession planning, compensation (equity, bonus, merit), mobility, change management, and other talent related initiatives.

Serve as a project lead for ES programs, responsible for tracking project objectives, timelines, and communication plans by prioritizing responsibilities, and tracking and measuring to successful completion, sometimes organizing other members of the ESPC team to achieve objectives.

Partner with leadership to drive organizational effectiveness and a high-performance culture.

Partner to support the business through transformation and change.

Executes on continued reshaping, growth, acquisition, reorganization, etc.

Work directly with people leaders and employees to coach and enable on a variety of ES topics and programs, resolve issues, etc.

Creates tools, templates and resources for consistent service delivery at scale.

Partner with ES Operations team on knowledge transfer and resolving root cause service delivery issues that impact the employee and manager experience.

Lead initiatives in partnership with COEs and PS&O peers to identify bodies of work that can be better done at scale through automation or revised processes and/or make recommendations when it is appropriate to align with different teams.

Requirements and Key Skills:

Experience working closely with ESBP, COEs, Executives, Managers, and Employees to handle a variety of issues and execute on solutions and strategies in all areas of Human Resources.

Demonstrated ability to support diverse, geographically dispersed, multicultural fast paced environment.

Demonstrated ability to be a self-starter and proactive.

Comfort with ambiguity and proven agent for change.

Ability to develop effective relationships across all levels of the organization with diverse client groups.

Excellent verbal and written communication skills; must be a strong and succinct communicator.

Demonstrated ability to maintain confidentiality in all aspects of job responsibilities, including handling highly sensitive data.

Ability to ramp up quickly, recognize Salesforce's unique culture and proactively help identify issues/areas of development.

Ability to collect, assimilate, analyze, and present data. Must have strong knowledge of Google Drive (Sheets and Slides) or other spreadsheet and presentations software, Workday, and Tableau.

Ability to take trends and data, connecting the dots and translating them into key insights and presentable themes.

Ability to partner with COEs to design, build, and implement programs and processes to support business needs.

Ability to deliver difficult feedback, coach leaders through difficult scenarios, and facilitate team discussions.

General knowledge of employment policies and practices.

Knowledge of staffing, compensation, and talent management best practices.

 

Requirements - Education & Experience:

Bachelor's degree or equivalent relevant experience

What is an Account Solution Engineer?
Also known as a "Presales", "Solution Advisor", "Sales Consultant," or "Sales Engineer" the Account Solution Engineer is responsible for owning the solutions throughout the sales cycle and delivering thought leadership to companies to transform their customer's experience. The Solution Engineer is an expert who works alongside their colleagues, participates in building our team culture and supports their aligned sales team to create solutions for prospects and customers.

To accomplish this, you should have a strong desire to set the vision translating business objectives into enticing and practical recommendations. The Solution Engineer acts as a trusted advisor to our customers while aiding the customer's transformational journey.

Responsibilities

Strategic Customer Engagement: Partner with sales teams to drive digital transformation initiatives for retail and consumer goods enterprises, positioning Salesforce as the platform for the agentic enterprise where AI agents work alongside humans to drive business outcomes

Solution Architecture & Design: Lead technical discovery sessions to understand complex business processes across retail operations, omnichannel commerce, supplier networks, and customer experience, translating requirements into comprehensive Salesforce solutions spanning across the portfolio

Agentic AI Evangelism: Demonstrate and articulate how Agentforce and autonomous AI agents can transform customer-facing and internal operations—from intelligent service agents handling customer inquiries to autonomous SDR agents qualifying leads and autonomous sales coaches optimizing rep performance

Value-Based Selling: Build and present compelling business cases that connect technical solutions to measurable business outcomes, focusing on revenue growth through platform expansion, cross-cloud opportunities, and demonstrating clear ROI

Demo/Workshop/Proof of Concept Execution: Design and deliver impactful demonstrations, customer workshops and pilots that showcase the art of the possible, driving operational efficiency and customer engagement in retail and consumer goods contexts

Trusted Advisor Relationship Building: Establish yourself as a strategic technology advisor to C-level and senior technical stakeholders, understanding their industry challenges around unified commerce, loyalty management, demand forecasting, supply chain visibility, and evolving consumer expectations

Platform Expansion & Land-and-Expand Strategy: Identify opportunities to expand Salesforce footprint within existing accounts by uncovering new use cases across departments and business units, driving new revenue through strategic platform growth

Collaboration & Knowledge Sharing: Work closely with product specialists, industry advisors, architects, customer success teams and partners to ensure seamless handoffs and contribute to the broader solution engineering community through knowledge sharing and best practice documentation

Key Attributes & Experience

Solution Engineering Excellence: Proven track record as a pre-sales solution engineer, sales engineer, or technical consultant successfully supporting complex software sales cycles

Business Acumen: Demonstrated ability to think strategically about business value rather than just technical features, with experience building business cases, ROI models, and value realization frameworks that resonate with executive buyers

Growth Mindset: Intellectual curiosity and commitment to continuous learning in a rapidly evolving technology landscape, particularly around AI/ML, data platforms, and industry-specific innovations

Customer-Centric: Consultative approach with exceptional listening skills and the ability to ask insightful discovery questions that uncover both stated and unstated customer needs

AI & Automation Fluency: Understanding of how artificial intelligence, machine learning, and autonomous agents are reshaping business processes, with ability to translate technical AI capabilities into business outcomes

Communication skills: Polished communication and presentation skills with the confidence to engage C-suite executives, technical architects, and operational managers alike, adapting your message and medium to each audience

Collaborative Team Player: Experience working in matrix organizations, partnering effectively with account executives, customer success managers, product teams, technical architects as well as external partners to drive customer outcomes

Language Skills: Swedish and Professional English proficiency required. Salesforce company language is English incl. corporate communication and meetings.

Proven Track Record: History of meeting or exceeding sales quotas through technical contribution, with metrics demonstrating impact on win rates, deal size, and sales cycle velocity

Preferred Skills and Experience

Industry Expertise: Experience in retail or consumer goods sectors, with understanding of industry-specific challenges e.g. omnichannel commerce, loyalty and personalization programs, demand planning, supplier collaboration, and the shift toward direct-to-consumer and unified commerce models

Prior experience in Business, Computer Science, MIS, or equivalent fields

Hands-on experience with Salesforce platform configuration, including exposure to Flow, Lightning Web Components, or Apex development

Previous experience and/or certification in Salesforce solutions such as Sales Cloud, Service Cloud, Commerce Cloud, Marketing Cloud, Loyalty Management, or B2B/B2C Commerce

Understanding of modern data architecture concepts including data lakes, real-time integration, API management, and master data management

Experience with AI/ML platforms, prompt engineering, or implementing intelligent automation solutions

Knowledge of retail industry standards and technologies (e.g., POS systems, ERP integrations, product information management, and e-commerce platforms)

Exposure to MuleSoft, Tableau, or other Salesforce ecosystem products

Experience with value engineering methodologies, business case development tools, or value realization frameworks

Previous experience selling or implementing large-scale ERP, CRM, or digital transformation platforms (SAP, Oracle, Microsoft Dynamics)

Understanding of European data privacy regulations (GDPR) and retail industry compliance requirements

Project management or Agile methodology certifications

Experience in presenting and hosting webinars or public speaking

Active participation in Salesforce community, Trailhead accomplishments, or speaking engagements at industry events

Fluent in Swedish and English + Right to work in Sweden

**Candidate must possess a Top Secret Security Clearance** 

Salesforce Public Sector Professional Services is currently looking for a Facility Security Officer (FSO) to join our growing team! In this role, the FSO will be responsible for the day to day management, oversight, and compliance of the Public Sector Professional Services industrial security program in accordance with all NISPOM and other applicable federal regulations. The FSO will manage security activities across a diverse portfolio of programs and ensure adherence to government regulations. In addition, this role will work closely with cross functional teams to develop, implement, and maintain security policies, procedures, and training programs for employees handling classified information. They will assist in investigations involving security violations and incidents, submitting reports to the appropriate agencies and implementing mitigation strategies.

Specific responsibilities include:

Ensure the protection of classified information, Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI), and sensitive customer data across Public Sector programs

Provide guidance to Public Sector consultants and leadership on industrial security best practices to adhere to all DCSA, NISPOM and other Government regulations

Serve as the primary liaison with Government security officials and internal stakeholders to maintain audit readiness, enable compliant delivery, and mitigate security risk across a diverse line of business.

Advise senior leadership on security risk posture and regulatory changes impacting Public Sector delivery.

Develop, implement, and maintain security policies, procedures, and training programs for employees handling classified information

Conduct self-inspections and preparation for Government audits. Identify security risks and deficiencies and recommend corrective actions.

Create and administer initial and recurring security awareness education for cleared staff in accordance with NISPOM and customer requirements. Track and report on training completion

Prepare and deliver regular written and verbal communications and updates for cleared personnel on relevant topics of interest.

Maintain security documentation and files in accordance with government requirements.

Ensure compliance with CMMC requirements and DFARS 252.204-7012 safeguarding and reporting obligations.

Maintain the Facility Clearance (FCL) in good standing, including KMP management, NISS reporting, and required DCSA submissions.

Administer security briefings and debriefings, foreign travel reporting, insider threat coordination, and security incident/spillage reporting in accordance with NISPOM and customer requirements.

Required Qualifications:

US Citizenship Required

Must possess and be able to maintain a Top Secret (TS) Clearance

FSO Program Management for Possessing Facilities curriculum 

Extensive knowledge and demonstrated experience in managing security programs in compliance with 32 CFR Part 117 (NISPOM) and Intelligence Community Directives (ICDs)

Thorough understanding of personnel security processes and best practices, including clearance processing, onboarding, and reinvestigations.

Proven ability to conduct self-inspections and prepare for government audits

Excellent organizational, communication, and leadership skills. Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail in implementing security policies and procedures.

Strong interpersonal communication and customer service skills are required in order to work successfully with internal and external stakeholders

Ability to be adaptive to an evolving environment

Experience supporting the Department of War, Intelligence Community, Federal Civilian, and other US Federal agencies.

Extensive knowledge and experience utilizing NBIS, NISS, DISS and other government applications

CUI Training, Personally Identifiable Information (PII) Training, and Cyber Awareness Training, must all be completed by the first day of employment.

Nice to Haves:

Previous experience working in a Professional Services organization or federal contracting environment.

Experience operating and maintaining an Insider Threat Program

Familiarity with security automation tools and audit readiness frameworks

Experience supporting multi-site or multi-entity cleared environments

Knowledge and experience utilizing ACCS preferred 

Education/Experience:
 

Bachelor’s degree or equivalent work experience in Industrial Security

3+ years experience in AFSO or FSO role, including work with classified programs and Government contracts; ISSM/ISSO experience desired

This candidate must be a U.S. citizen (U.S. born or naturalized) who does not hold dual citizenship and agrees to complete a U.S. federal government Minimum Background Investigation (MBI) for a Moderate Public Trust position.This position requires a USA Top Secret security access level.

The team is the “voice of the customer and market” to the rest of the CS organization and we work across all functions within CS and the Salesforce product organizations to continuously enhance the customer value and experience delivered by the Success Plans offer portfolio.

Position Overview

The Senior Product Manager is responsible for driving enhancements to the Salesforce Signature Success Plan offer. As a key member of the product team, you will help define the product roadmap, and work with other product managers, and key stakeholder teams in sales, delivery, marketing, finance and operations to bring products to market. The ideal candidate will have a deep understanding of the Salesforce platform, the needs of Salesforce customers, and the specific requirements of the Signature Success Plan offer.

Help define and execute on the product roadmap for Signature Success Plans and ensure alignment with business goals

Work with delivery, sales, and marketing to bring products to market

Excellent understanding of the Salesforce platform

English Version:

About Salesforce SPM:
Salesforce Sales Performance Management (SPM) is a strategic product suite within the Sales Cloud portfolio designed to help the world’s leading enterprises bridge the gap between strategy and execution. By leveraging a unified platform for Go-To-Market (GTM) strategy, AI-powered coaching, and automated Incentive Compensation Management, SPM empowers organizations to optimize their sales force's productivity. It serves as a mission-critical layer of the Salesforce ecosystem, turning complex sales data into actionable insights that drive team motivation and predictable revenue growth.

As a core driver of the Salesforce "Growth" strategy, SPM integrates seamlessly with our broader cloud offerings to modernize the entire sales lifecycle. From refining territory and quota planning to ensuring precise, real-time compensation visibility, our SPM solutions enable Enterprise leaders to pivot their strategies with agility. By joining this team, you will be at the forefront of helping customers transition from manual, fragmented processes to a sophisticated, tech-enabled sales culture that maximizes every seller's potential.

Duties and Responsibilities:

Manage a full-lifecycle sales process, prospecting customers via phone, email, referrals, LinkedIn, Salesforce relationships, partners, etc.

Identify appropriate business contacts, qualify and drive leads through the sales pipeline

Engage in technical discussions with potential clients through product demonstrations and presentations with Director, VP, and C Level prospects

Manage accounts and contacts within Salesforce.com through the entire sales lifecycle

Respond to customer inquiries and requests

Establish and maintain long-term relationships to enhance future revenue opportunities

Ability to match our sales process with customer buying processes

Able to be in territory 1-2 times per month, up to 4-5 days at a time

Desired Skills:

7+ years of technology based sales experience, enterprise experience is preferred

B2B/B2C SaaS or cloud enterprise sales experience

Deep understanding of sales operations, compensation, and GTM strategy

Ability to conduct consultative, solution-based selling (not just product features)

Track record of managing complex enterprise sales cycles

Strategic thinking with ability to prioritize based on business impact

Adaptable approach to achieve objectives in dynamic environments

Ability to multi-task, take initiative, prioritize, and manage time effectively

Bachelor’s degree in Business, Communication, Marketing or related field is strongly preferred
 

Preferred Requirements:

Experience selling sales productivity, incentive compensation, or sales operations technology

Salesforce experience or administrator certification

Excellent presentation and executive communication skills

Background in GTM strategy, sales operations, or revenue operations

Experience as a solution evangelist or thought leader

French Version:
Salesforce Sales Performance Management (SPM) est une suite de produits stratégiques au sein du portefeuille Sales Cloud, conçue pour aider les plus grandes entreprises mondiales à combler le fossé entre la stratégie et l'exécution. En s'appuyant sur une plateforme unifiée pour la stratégie de mise sur le marché (GTM), le coaching alimenté par l'IA et la gestion automatisée des commissions (Incentive Compensation Management), SPM permet aux organisations d'optimiser la productivité de leur force de vente. Véritable couche critique de l'écosystème Salesforce, elle transforme des données de vente complexes en informations exploitables qui stimulent la motivation des équipes et une croissance prévisible des revenus.

En tant que moteur central de la stratégie de croissance de Salesforce, SPM s'intègre parfaitement à nos offres cloud plus larges pour moderniser l'ensemble du cycle de vie de la vente. De l'affinement de la planification des secteurs et des quotas à la visibilité précise et en temps réel des rémunérations, nos solutions SPM permettent aux dirigeants d'entreprises de faire pivoter leurs stratégies avec agilité. En rejoignant cette équipe, vous serez aux avant-postes pour aider les clients à passer de processus manuels et fragmentés à une culture de vente sophistiquée et technologique qui maximise le potentiel de chaque vendeur.

Missions et Responsabilités :

Gérer l'intégralité du cycle de vente, de la prospection (via téléphone, e-mail, recommandations, LinkedIn, relations Salesforce, partenaires, etc.) jusqu'à la conclusion.

Identifier les contacts commerciaux pertinents, qualifier et faire progresser les prospects à travers le pipeline de vente.

Mener des discussions techniques avec des clients potentiels par le biais de démonstrations de produits et de présentations auprès d'interlocuteurs de niveau Directeur, Vice-Président et C-Level (Direction Générale).

Gérer les comptes et les contacts au sein de Salesforce.com tout au long du cycle de vente.

Répondre aux demandes et sollicitations des clients.

Établir et maintenir des relations à long terme afin de favoriser les opportunités de revenus futurs.

Adapter notre processus de vente aux processus d'achat des clients.

Compétences Requises :

7 ans d'expérience minimum dans la vente de technologies, de préférence dans le secteur des grandes entreprises (Enterprise).

Expérience en vente B2B/B2C SaaS ou cloud aux grandes entreprises (Enterprise)

Compréhension approfondie des opérations de vente (Sales Ops), de la rémunération variable et des stratégies de mise sur le marché (GTM).

Capacité à mener une vente consultative orientée solutions (et non seulement sur les fonctionnalités du produit).

Expérience dans la gestion de cycles de vente complexes pour des grandes entreprises.

Esprit stratégique avec une capacité à prioriser les actions en fonction de l'impact commercial.

Approche adaptable pour atteindre les objectifs dans des environnements dynamiques et complexes.

Capacité à gérer plusieurs tâches de front, à prendre des initiatives, à prioriser et à gérer son temps efficacement.

Licence ou Master en commerce, communication, marketing ou dans un domaine connexe fortement souhaité.

Qualifications Souhaitées :

Expérience dans la vente de solutions de productivité commerciale, de gestion des commissions ou de technologies pour les opérations de vente.

Expérience sur Salesforce ou certification d'administrateur.

Excellentes compétences en présentation et en communication auprès de cadres dirigeants.

Expérience préalable en stratégie GTM, en Sales Operations ou en Revenue Operations (RevOps).

Expérience en tant qu'évangéliste de solutions ou leader d'opinion.

As a leader on this team, you will play a pivotal role in shaping strategy, managing team capacity, aligning with key stakeholders, and enabling scalable impact across our customer base.In this leadership position, you will be responsible for setting the strategic direction and operational cadence of the Success Architect function. As a Senior Manager for Tableau Success Architects, you will ensure optimal capacity planning and resource alignment across a portfolio of critical initiatives and high-impact customer engagements within the Analytics product area. 

You will lead a geographically distributed team while fostering strong relationships across the broader Success organization, Sales, Customer Success, and Product teams. You will create an inclusive team culture that values diverse perspectives, encourages knowledge sharing, and drives collective success across regions and functions.

This is a people leadership position.

Your Impact

As a Senior Manager of Tableau Success Architects, you will directly impact customer success at scale by building and leading a high-performing team that drives measurable outcomes for our most strategic customers. Your leadership will shape how we deliver advisory services, demonstrate value, and grow our Success Architect practice for Tableau.

Join our dynamic leadership team and help shape the future of how we deliver world-class advisory services to our most strategic Tableau customers. We embrace diversity and are committed to creating equal opportunities for all applicants. If you are passionate about leading technical teams, driving operational excellence, demonstrating measurable impact, and fostering inclusive cultures, we invite you to apply for this Senior Manager position.

Minimum Requirements

10+ years of experience in Customer Success, Solution Architecture, Technical Consulting, or related fields.

5+ years of people management experience, including leading geographically distributed teams

Proven ability to manage schedules, shifts, and capacity planning. You should be comfortable using data to argue for resources or process changes.

Familiarity with Tableau implementations, architecture, and optimization strategies

Proven track record driving teams to operational metrics (delivery velocity, time to resolve, customer satisfaction)

Experience in enterprise consulting or advisory services

Ability to leverage data to identify opportunities to drive outsized impact with target customers

Experience developing methods to measure impact in advisory engagements

Demonstrated expertise in coaching technical professionals

Outstanding communication and presentation skills for C-level audiences

Preferred Qualifications

Experience managing teams supporting multiple product lines or technical domains

Background in building or scaling advisory, professional services, or customer success programs

Familiarity with Salesforce Core, Data Cloud, CRM Analytics, and cross-cloud solutions

Knowledge of modern data platforms (Snowflake, Databricks, Redshift) and analytics tools

Track record of developing methodology frameworks for advisory practices

Preferred Certifications: Advanced Admin, Sales/Service Cloud Consultant, AI Associate, Data360, Agentblazer, Tableau: Architect, Consultant Data Analyst, Desktop Specialist, Server Associate.

Note: this role is office-flexible, and the expectation is to be in-office a minimum of three (3) days per week.
